mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no significant negative correlation with FEDFUNDS changes (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5%, borrowing costs for corporations and consumers become restrictive enough to suppress capital expenditure and consumption, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >55% of periods where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, or the threshold signal loses predictive power (t-stat < 1.5) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in the unemployment rate and an increase in total nonfarm payrolls indicates strengthening labor market absorption, which boosts consumer spending expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce positive forward returns in >60% of triggered instances, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery phases, rising retail sales and housing starts signal rapid demand normalization, which triggers short-term momentum buying and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative forward returns in >50% of post-shock recovery periods, or the predictive alpha decays to zero within 3 trading days.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production reflects increasing corporate output and capacity utilization, which translates into higher near-term earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth shows no significant positive correlation with 20-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0.01) across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth falls below 0.5%, aggregate demand contraction triggers earnings downgrades and risk-off positioning, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >65% of periods where GDPC1 growth is below 0.5%, or the threshold effect disappears in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising federal funds rates and accelerating consumer price inflation forces markets to price in aggressive monetary tightening, causing immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint inflation-rate signal fails to predict negative returns in >55% of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In post-shock recovery regimes, a declining unemployment rate signals successful labor market stabilization, which reduces recession risk premiums and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal yields negative forward returns in >60% of post-shock recovery periods, or the predictive power vanishes when controlling for monetary policy shifts.
